title: Conagra: Attractive Valuation For This Dividend Grower
text: Conagra delivered a mixed fourth quarter, but they topped expectations for the fiscal year. CAG has been able to push through price increases to consumers and has seen improving margins. In this latest quarter, they also announced a dividend increase, which is starting to create a trend showing a more reliable dividend growth consistency.

title: Orville Redenbacher's Debuts New Popcorn Seasonings
text: Six sweet and savory flavors pair perfectly with America's Top Selling Popcorn CHICAGO , July 25, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Ever since Orville Redenbacher brought his first popcorn kernels to market, his namesake brand has been responsible for countless warm, flavorful snacking moments. That flavor is headed to the next level as Orville Redenbacher's, a brand of Conagra Brands, Inc., (NYSE: CAG) debuts Orville Redenbacher's Popcorn Seasonings.

title: Bear of the Day: Conagra (CAG)
text: Conagra Brands, Inc. CAG has been fighting inflation over the last year. This Zacks Rank #5 (Strong Sell) recently guided below the Zacks Consensus for fiscal 2024.

title: Conagra Brands Announces Sizzling Summer Line-up of New Products
text: Frozen, grocery and snacks offerings tap into latest trends with more than 50 items CHICAGO , July 19, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Conagra Brands, Inc. (NYSE: CAG), one of North America's leading branded food companies, has unveiled more than 50 new products this summer across the company's frozen, grocery and snacks divisions. As consumer needs evolve, Conagra's iconic brands have crafted new recipes that deliver contemporary, flavorful food in dynamic ways.

title: Like Growing Dividend Income? This Blue Chip Stock Just Upped Its Payout by 6%
text: Conagra's brand prowess could power solid earnings growth moving forward. The company's market-trouncing dividend could keep growing at a moderate pace.

title: 4 Stocks to Watch on Dividend Hikes as Inflation Fears Linger
text: The J. M. Smucker Company (SJM), Avient Corporation (AVNT), AptarGroup, Inc. (ATR) and Conagra Brands, Inc. (CAG) recently hiked dividends.

title: Conagra CEO notes 'hunkering down' behavior in grocery shoppers
text: Conagra Brands CEO Sean Connolly on Thursday revealed a 'likely temporary' behavior shift the company has observed from shoppers: 'hunkering down' over 'trading down.'

title: Deep Value Conagra Brands Pulls Into The Buy Zone
text: Conagra Brands NASDAQ: CAG isn't leading the consumer staples sector but is a leading candidate for new investment. The company's struggles have the share prices trading near multi-year lows and offering a deep value to investors.

title: Conagra Is A Deeply Undervalued Dividend Play
text: Despite a 13% drop in stock price since May, Conagra Brands' recent earnings indicate a path to future strength, with improved margins and solid sales growth in most segments. The company expects organic net sales growth, increased adjusted operating margin, and improved supply chain operations in the fiscal year 2024.

title: Conagra (CAG) Q4 Earnings Top Estimates, Decline Y/Y
text: Conagra's (CAG) fourth-quarter results reflect lower earnings on increased costs. Nevertheless, sales increased year over year on favorable pricing action.

title: Consumers are now ‘hunkering down' rather than ‘trading down' on groceries, Conagra says
text: Consumers are no longer simply looking for cheaper food brands to stretch their grocery budget. Now they are actually buying less food, according to Sean Connolly, CEO of consumer-foods giant Conagra Brands Inc.

title: Conagra Brands posts 4Q profit beat but soft fiscal 2024 guidance disappoints
text: Conagra Brands posted a fourth quarter profit beat but its stock failed to gain momentum as it also issued a soft fiscal 2024 outlook. The American consumer packaged goods company, whose major brands include Hunt's, Slim Jim, Pam, Orville Redenbacher's, and Chef Boyardee, reported adjusted earnings per share (EPS) for 4Q fiscal 2023 of $0.62, above the Street's expectation of $0.60.

title: Conagra Brands (CAG) Reports Q4 Earnings: What Key Metrics Have to Say
text: Although the revenue and EPS for Conagra Brands (CAG) give a sense of how its business performed in the quarter ended May 2023, it might be worth considering how some key metrics compare with Wall Street estimates and the year-ago numbers.

title: Conagra Sales Rise, Profit Falls as Inflation Eases
text: The food manufacturer said shoppers continued to stomach rising food prices, boosting its margins and sales.

title: Conagra Brands (CAG) Tops Q4 Earnings Estimates
text: Conagra Brands (CAG) came out with quarterly earnings of $0.62 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.60 per share. This compares to earnings of $0.65 per share a year ago.

title: Conagra Brands Beats Earnings Estimates, Boosts Dividend. Why the Stock Is Falling.
text: The processed food maker posted fourth-quarter adjusted earnings that beat expectations, a dividend boost, and disappointing guidance.

title: Conagra raises dividend, to lift implied yield above 4.2%
text: Conagra Brands Inc.'s said Thursday that it has raised its quarterly dividend by 6.1%, to 35 cents a share from 33 cents a share, Based on the branded foods company's stock closing price of $32.98 on Wednesday, the new annual dividend rate implies a dividend yield of 4.24%, which compares with the yield for the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR exchange-traded fund XLP, +0.23% of 2.53% and the implied yield for the S&P 500 SPX, +0.74% of 1.53%. Conagra's stock fell 1% toward a nine-month low in premarket trading, after the company, with food brands including Hunt's, Reddi Whip, Healthy Choice and Vlasic, reported fiscal fourth-quarter revenue that came up shy of expectations and provided a downbeat full-year profit view.

title: Conagra stock falls toward 9-month low after revenue comes up shy, full-year profit view was below forecasts
text: Shares of Conagra Brands Inc. CAG, -0.42% slid 0.9% toward a nine-month low in premarket trading Thursday, after the branded foods company's fiscal fourth-quarter revenue fell short of forecasts and the full-year outlook was downbeat. The company, with brands including Birds Eye, Slim Jim, Duncan Hines and Hunt's, said net income for the quarter to May 28 fell to $37.5 million, or 8 cents a share, from $158.9 million, or 33 cents a share, in the year-ago period.

title: Conagra: Cautiously Reconsider This Stock (Rating Upgrade)
text: Conagra Brands is considered a buy for long-term investors due to its potential for generating income. The company's lack of volume growth is a concern, with sales volumes declining by 9% y/y in Q3 2023. Despite a high debt load, Conagra offers a good dividend yield and potential for dividend growth; the stock is considered good value at $33 or below.

title: Earnings Preview: Conagra Brands (CAG) Q4 Earnings Expected to Decline
